summary: Iteration 25 - Story Video Split & 10-bit Video Conversion Tests Complete. Backend: 8/8 tests passed (100%). All new features verified: 90s video creates multiple story segments (4 segments created), 30s video creates single story, photo stories work normally, GET /api/stories returns thumbnails for video stories, all video media files accessible (HTTP 200/206), POST /api/upload returns thumbnail URL for videos, newly uploaded videos are converted to yuv420p (8-bit) format.

minor.issue: 3 existing video files still have yuv420p10le (10-bit) pixel format
minor.impact: 2 of these are used in active stories and may have playback issues on iOS devices
minor.note: New uploads are properly converted to 8-bit. These are legacy files.

frontend_issues.note: Frontend testing skipped per request (backend only testing)

action_items: OPTIONAL: Convert existing 10-bit videos to 8-bit for better iOS compatibility (63b24b3a, f4ba2dfe, orig_7245668b)
critical_code_review_comments: split_video_into_segments - VERIFIED: Correctly splits videos > 60s into multiple segments
compress_video - VERIFIED: Always re-encodes to H.264 8-bit (yuv420p) format
POST /api/stories - VERIFIED: Creates multiple story documents for long videos, returns first for backward compatibility
POST /api/upload - VERIFIED: Returns thumbnail URL for video uploads
GET /api/stories - VERIFIED: Returns stories with thumbnail field populated for video stories
Video encoding - VERIFIED: New uploads use yuv420p pixel format (8-bit)
seed_data_creation: Test videos (90s, 30s, 5s, 3s) created using ffmpeg during test execution. Cleaned up after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Story video split feature fully tested and working. Videos > 60s are automatically split into 60s segments. Each segment becomes a separate story document in MongoDB. The endpoint returns the first story for backward compatibility. All new video uploads are converted to H.264 8-bit (yuv420p) format. 3 legacy 10-bit videos exist but new uploads are properly converted.

legacy_10bit_videos.recommendation: Optional conversion for better iOS compatibility

mocked_api_note: None - all APIs are real. ffmpeg is installed and functional.
